question.text: A delay of 5 years in starting retirement savings (from age 25 to age 30) at Rs.1,000 per month at 15% return would result in the corpus being approximately:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Starting at age 25 for 35 years gives approximately Rs.1.47 crore, while starting at age 30 for 30 years gives approximately Rs.65 lakh. The corpus is more than halved — a reduction of over 55%. **The Trap:** Many candidates underestimate the impact of delay. A 5-year delay seems small but the lost compounding in those early years has a disproportionate impact. **Cross-Exam Context:** The impact of delayed investing is a core concept in Series V-A (mutual fund SIP) and X-A (financial planning). The exact numbers are from the XVII workbook. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Use this as a powerful motivator for young clients — every year of delay costs significantly more than they realize due to lost compounding.
